title: annona haitiensis subsp. haitiensis
intro: Annona haitiensis subsp. haitiensis, also known as the Haitian custard apple or West Indian cherry, is a plant species native to Haiti, belonging to the Annonaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of Annona haitiensis subsp. haitiensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use in treating gastrointestinal issues and as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, seeds, and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
